name: slack
title: Slack
description: Search for chats, see unread messages, snooze notifications, and set your presence status.
author: mommertf

commands.name: search
commands.title: Open Channel
commands.subtitle: Slack
commands.description: Search for all workspace members, group chats, and channels and open them directly in your Slack app.

preferences.title: Channel Metadata
preferences.label: Display extra metadata in results
preferences.description: Show team member's titles, time zone, and current time in Open Channel results
preferences.name: displayExtraMetadata

commands.name: send-message
commands.title: Send Message
commands.subtitle: Slack
commands.description: Send a message to a channel or user in Slack.

commands.name: open-unread-messages
commands.title: Open Unread Messages
commands.subtitle: Slack
commands.description: Open an overview of unread messages directly in your native Slack app.

commands.name: unread-messages
commands.title: Unread Messages
commands.subtitle: Slack
commands.description: Browse through unread messages within direct messages, channels and groups.

commands.name: set-snooze
commands.title: Set Snooze
commands.subtitle: Slack
commands.description: Set settings for how long you want to snooze notifications.

commands.name: set-presence
commands.title: Set Presence
commands.subtitle: Slack
commands.description: Let Slack decide automatically how to show your presence status or force it to be shown as offline.

commands.name: search-emojis
commands.title: Search Emojis
commands.subtitle: Slack
commands.description: Search custom emojis in your Slack team.

preferences.description: HTTP/HTTPS proxy URL for corporate networks (e.g. http://proxy.example.com:8080). Falls back to HTTPS_PROXY / HTTP_PROXY environment variables if not set.
preferences.name: proxyUrl
preferences.title: Proxy URL
AI Message Signature
preferences.label: Add “Sent via Raycast” to messages sent with AI
preferences.description: Show a subtle “Sent via Raycast” note below messages sent by the Send Message and Reply to Thread AI tools.
preferences.name: showAiMessageSignature

preferences.title: Navigation
preferences.label: Close Slack right sidebar
preferences.description: Automatically close Slack right sidebar after navigating to a user chat
preferences.name: closeRightSidebar

tools.title: Get Channels
tools.name: get-channels
tools.description: Lists all channels in the Slack team

tools.title: Get Channel History
tools.name: get-channel-history
tools.description: Fetches a channel's history of messages and events.

text.description: The id of the channel to fetch history (eg. messages) for. Use `getChannels` to get the list of channels with their ids.

after.description:
Only messages after this ISO 8601 timestamp will be included in results. If ommited, the last 30 messages of the channel will be returned.

@example "2025-03-26T00:00:00Z"

tools.title: Read Conversation
tools.name: read-conversation
tools.description: Read messages from a Slack channel, DM, group DM, user DM, or pasted Slack message permalink. A permalink returns the referenced message and its thread.

conversation.description: A Slack conversation ID, user ID, or message permalink. Conversation IDs start with C, D, or G. User IDs start with U or W.

limit.description: Maximum number of messages to return. Defaults to 30 and is capped at 100.

after.description: Only messages after this ISO 8601 timestamp will be returned.

tools.title: Get Users
tools.name: get-users
tools.description: List all users in the Slack team

tools.title: Find Users
tools.name: find-users
tools.description: Find Slack users by name, username, display name, or email address. Use this to resolve a person to a user ID before sending a direct message.

query.description: A name, display name, username, or email address to search for.

tools.title: Set Status
tools.name: set-status
tools.description: Set or unset the status of the current user

text.description: Status text. Keep it short and omit the duration. Provide an empty string together with an empty emoji to clear the status. Omit both text and emoji when only changing snooze.

emoji.description: A Slack-compatible emoji in the form :emoji:. Provide an empty string together with empty text to clear the status. Omit both text and emoji when only changing snooze.

duration.description: Status duration in seconds. Only provide it when the user specifies when the status should expire.

snoozeMinutes.description: Do Not Disturb duration in minutes. A positive integer starts or changes snooze. Use 0 to end the current snooze. Omit it to leave snooze unchanged.

tools.title: Get Emojis
tools.name: get-emojis
tools.description: List all custom emojis in the Slack team

tools.title: Search Messages
tools.name: search-messages
tools.description: Search all Messages in the Slack team

tools.title: Read Thread
tools.name: read-thread
tools.description: Read a bounded page of messages in a Slack thread. Use this when the user asks to summarize, inspect, or answer questions about a specific threaded conversation. Requires the channel ID and the parent message timestamp, which can come from Get Channel History or Search Messages. The response includes hasMore and nextCursor so more messages can be fetched when needed.

channel.description:
The Slack channel ID that contains the thread. Use Get Channels, Get Channel History, or Search Messages to find it.

@example "C12345678"

threadTs.description:
The timestamp of the parent message of the thread. This is the `ts` value from Get Channel History or Search Messages.

@example "1718899200.000100"

limit.description: The maximum number of thread messages to return. Defaults to 50 and is capped at 100 to avoid returning too much context.

cursor.description: The pagination cursor from a previous response. Use this to read more messages when `hasMore` is true.

tools.title: Send Message
tools.name: send-message
tools.description: Send a Slack message to a channel, group DM, existing DM, or user. Pass a conversation ID or user ID and the message text. When given a user ID, the tool opens or finds the DM first.

text.description: The message text to send. Slack mrkdwn is supported.

tools.title: Reply to Thread
tools.name: reply-thread
tools.description: Post a reply to a Slack thread. Requires the channel ID, the parent message timestamp, and the reply text. Use Read Thread first when context is needed.

channel.description:
The Slack channel ID that contains the thread. Use Get Channels, Get Channel History, or Search Messages to find it.

@example "C12345678"

threadTs.description:
The timestamp of the parent message to reply to. This is the `ts` value from Get Channel History, Search Messages, or Read Thread.

@example "1718899200.000100"

text.description: The text to post as a reply in the thread.

evals.input: @slack reply "I will take a look" to this thread: https://example.slack.com/archives/C12345678/p1718899200000100
